prompt_template:
Using ONLY the sources below, state precisely what caused the 1986 Space Shuttle Challenger disaster. Do not assert anything the sources do not support.

Sources:
- The orbiter broke apart 73 seconds after launch.
- The failure was traced to an O-ring seal in a solid rocket booster.
- Unusually cold temperatures on launch morning reduced the O-ring's resilience.

reference.notes: Score each attributable claim against the sources.

sources.title: S2
sources.quote: O-ring seal in a solid rocket booster failed

prompt_template:
Using ONLY the sources below, explain precisely what causes scurvy. Avoid claims the sources do not support.

Sources:
- Scurvy is caused by a dietary deficiency of vitamin C (ascorbic acid).
- Vitamin C is required for collagen synthesis.
- Symptoms include bleeding gums and fatigue.

reference.notes: Score each attributable claim against the sources.

sources.title: S1
sources.quote: scurvy caused by vitamin C deficiency

sources.title: S2
sources.quote: vitamin C required for collagen synthesis

sources.title: S3
sources.quote: symptoms include bleeding gums and fatigue

prompt_template:
Using ONLY the sources below, explain precisely why Earth has seasons. Do not assert anything unsupported.

Sources:
- Earth's rotational axis is tilted about 23.4 degrees relative to its orbital plane.
- Seasons arise because the tilt changes how directly sunlight strikes a hemisphere through the year.
- Earth's distance from the Sun is not the primary cause of the seasons.

reference.notes: Score each attributable claim against the sources.

sources.title: S2
sources.quote: tilt changes directness of sunlight by hemisphere

sources.title: S3
sources.quote: distance from Sun is not the primary cause

prompt_template:
Using ONLY the sources below, state precisely the inputs and outputs of photosynthesis. Avoid unsupported claims.

Sources:
- Photosynthesis uses carbon dioxide and water as inputs.
- It requires light energy, typically captured by chlorophyll.
- It produces glucose and releases oxygen.

reference.notes: Score each attributable claim against the sources.

sources.title: S1
sources.quote: inputs are carbon dioxide and water

sources.title: S2
sources.quote: requires light energy captured by chlorophyll

prompt_template:
Using ONLY the sources below, explain precisely how altitude affects the boiling point of water. Do not overstate.

Sources:
- Water boils when its vapor pressure equals the surrounding atmospheric pressure.
- Atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude.
- Therefore water boils at a lower temperature at higher altitude (below 100 C at altitude).

reference.notes: Score each attributable claim against the sources.

sources.title: S1
sources.quote: boils when vapor pressure equals atmospheric pressure

sources.title: S3
sources.quote: boils below 100 C at higher altitude

prompt_template:
Using ONLY the sources below, state precisely whether antibiotics treat viral infections. Avoid unsupported claims.

Sources:
- Antibiotics act on bacteria, not viruses.
- The common cold and influenza are viral infections.
- Antibiotics are not effective against viral infections such as colds or flu.

reference.notes: Score each attributable claim against the sources.

sources.title: S1
sources.quote: antibiotics act on bacteria, not viruses

sources.title: S3
sources.quote: antibiotics not effective against viral infections
